https://t.co/yFpFU4pn5b

Just launched GBrain v0.8.0 

If you have it installed, you can just ask your Claw/Hermes to upgrade to the latest GBrain and we'll automatically ask if you want to install your Voice WebRTC endpoint and Twilio number 

It's a true mega brain-trip to talk to your agent directly. https://t.co/EuxWIK07V4

If you are a developer/designer/PM and haven't experienced it yet, I want this for you https://t.co/sY5mzQxmMG
RT @beffjezos: Fun fact: I once saw some PauseAI screenshots discussing how they should drag my company's name in the mud in order to suppr…

This is more useful if you do what I do which is separate my knowledge wiki repo out from my core openclaw repo

^^ this has the added benefit of allowing your "brain memory" to live separately from your "OS" which means that multiple agents can use git to update your brain

One fun thing to do with your Claw: Code Reviews 

You can open your OpenClaw repo in Claude Code and manually code review its commits on its own repo to catch bugs and/or be more in the flow on what OpenClaw is doing to build itself on your behalf. 

Trust but verify! https://t.co/oy8r6z2Eoe

Get started here: https://t.co/yFpFU4pn5b

It's open source and MIT licensed, and we are a community of homebrew personal AI software builders who are just getting started.

If you are consistently hitting the limits of what the packaged AI software world can give you, then you owe it to yourself to try the personal AI software world 

Try OpenClaw/Hermes Agent, and you can skip ahead to my opinionated set of skills, schema, and memory with GBrain https://t.co/Ct2gbEhxnt

Freedom is so much more powerful than locked down guard rails

Free the Claw

(Still love Claude / Claude Code / Claude CoWork, but on the weekends I'm using my personal freedom agent!) https://t.co/6wqqfoW52O

Here's the actual GBrain Skillpack Recipe

https://t.co/2ErpaBwbxd 

This is one of my recent realizations: Just-in-Time software means YOUR Claw/Hermes will custom code something that works for YOU and your needs. 

This is Personal AI software, not packaged software.

A very early version of this is already built into GBrain's Skillpack, which you can install here 

We don't require Supabase API key anymore, so your time-to-having-Her in your Claw or Hermes is probably around 15 minutes now (still need Twilio/OpenAI)

https://t.co/yFpFU4pn5b

I'm having a Her moment over here with my OpenClaw. 

It's literally the craziest thing in the world to be able to just talk to your claw and have fully customizable voice. Did you ever want to improve your ChatGPT Voice experience? Now you can have it totally under your control https://t.co/v2SfNsCS9M

The most underrated thing in AI agents right now is: OpenClaw/Hermes Agent is just more free than other locked down AI agents (the standard out-of-box Claude/ChatGPT route)

"Free the Claw" is not a vibe I understood until I tried it. Now that I have it, I don't want to go back.

@chooserich Yes this is why the resolver concept is so important

If you're OpenClaw or Hermes Agent-pilled and want to build your own Karpathy Knowledge LLM but are stymied by integrations or need to stop using grep on 10,000 files, I open sourced my personal Claw/Hermes schema, skillpack and RAG memory system

https://t.co/yFpFU4pn5b

If you're Claude Code-pilled and want to go faster, check out my MIT-licensed open source project to create a software factory 

https://t.co/VPvWDzV5c0
@voxpraveen Continuous conversation, back and forth
https://t.co/EVPCod6rxu
@AradhyeAgarwal Yeah tbh talking with your claw by telephone is insanely cool

Update 7 hours later: All of this is now shipped
@emonuxui Cron jobs

This is now fixed! 

PGLite (embeddable WASM Postgres with vector support) is the default engine now, so no fumbling with API keys to get it going. https://t.co/aTfcblPJRC

New setup guide and new recommendations for fastest-to-get-your-claw-going even before you setup GBrain here

https://t.co/yFpFU4pn5b

I'm here to ship open source code and chew bubble gum

And I'm all out of bubble gum https://t.co/gnfJ8IAF4R

New OpenClaw and Hermes Agent users struggle to get all your integrations set up. 

And you can't get to 1,000 markdown files let alone 10,000 without proper integrations with mail, calendar etc.

GBrain now makes it very easy with guided integration recipes on first install https://t.co/z14kcnMmDF

MCP is hard to set up, but luckily you can just ask your OpenClaw or Hermes Agent how to get it working and it'll help you get all the way there. 

GBrain + your agent now helps you install all my actual workflow and ingestion process, not just pgvector

https://t.co/yFpFU4pn5b

GBrain MCP now works in ChatGPT as well

This one was the most crippled, as for some reason ChatGPT thinks MCP will exfiltrate your AI agent's memory. https://t.co/T4d4a28mvG

GBrain now works properly in Claude using GBrain's MCP server

Now your OpenClaw or Hermes Agent's memory and LLM Knowledge Wiki can be accessed and used by Claude, Claude Cowork and Claude Code by MCP https://t.co/R2XbY1xinP

RT @signulll: these dudes build & ship genuinely great stuff, in this case a fundamental shift in how we interface with our devices. 

aqua…

Thanks so much for using GBrain and making this magic thing work https://t.co/D1Gp53ZqWD

This screenshot is from the "Developer Experience Empathy Test" part of /plan-devex-review

This is what a truly great PM can do. Now anyone can have it. You can just do things now.

Yes, this is indeed a somewhat frustrating setup to get OpenClaw + GBrain + LLM Knowledge Wiki talking to your phone. 

These are all things that need to be fixed. We're in the Altair BASIC era of AI agents.

(Screenshot below from underrated GStack skill /plan-DevEx-review) https://t.co/3ljs0JfIDE

Two things I'm excited to ship for GBrain for the next 36 hours:

Connectors to Claude Cowork / Perplexity Computer so your GBrain can talk with the locked down agents via MCP

GBrain Skillpack that will let you have Call-Your-Claw-By-Phone via Twilio with ONE slash command
I'm in /office-hours mode now with GStack working on this, and it's definitely getting what I am putting down https://t.co/NQGWqr1TEG
This is the obvious consequence of models like Opus 4.6 1M that are smart enough and have enough context to just "figure it out!" https://t.co/wnxBQOqlQN
TBH I don't think I would have arrived at the idea of open source markdown-as-software recipes without spending two whole months building GStack

@anomsiiwa I dunno, GBrain will have a version system and then whatever happens on your repo is up to you and your claw

Here's a preview of what I'm going to refactor right now. You'll be able to pick and choose things that I made for your claw. 

You can do this manually right now just by pasting this MD into claw and saying "I want feature X and Y and Z" and it'll build it! https://t.co/RFOgSol0VR

This is all open source, and you can host it all yourself. 

As I build more things (handling photos, PDFs, more integrations) you'll get all of them as I go. 

GBrain will ping you when new features drop and ask you if you want them in your claw daily.

https://t.co/yFpFU4pn5b

PS I'm now working on a branch of GBrain that will allow you to get this whole functionality for your OpenClaw or Hermes Agent. 

This is what I mean by markdown is code. It will remember and create a tutorial that YOUR claw will use to implement it like I did, but for YOU and https://t.co/4xrMYOo37U
